transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: It is early winter in Bellwether: cold rain, grey skies, brisk wind and occasional frost.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The bus leaves you at Bellwether just after nine. Its engine noise thins along the road until the village is quiet again.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: For now, there is the road ahead, your suitcase at your feet, and Eleanor's key in your pocket.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You wait beneath the shelter. Traffic is sparse enough that every engine can be heard before it appears.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You wait beneath the shelter. Traffic is sparse enough that every engine can be heard before it appears.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: The road beyond the bus stop has begun to feel less like the way out and more like one edge of Bellwether.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.text: The weather has shifted: mist, 5°C, with still wind.
